task: I need to send a quick group chat message about a change in our school plan, and I want it to be clear enough that people know what’s happening. What’s a simple way to word it so I can choose the version that feels right?

turns.agent_response:
Here are a few simple options you can use, depending on the tone you want:

**Neutral:**  
“Quick update: there’s been a change to our school plan. I’ll share the details as soon as I have them.”

**More direct:**  
“Just a heads up — our school plan has changed. Please check for the updated details.”

**Friendly:**  
“Hey everyone, quick update: there’s been a change to our school plan. I’ll send more info soon.”

**Very short:**  
“School plan update: there’s been a change. More details soon.”

If you want, I can also make it sound **more formal, more casual, or more urgent**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. The short one is probably best if I just need it fast. The direct one is fine too.
